items.html: The seventh discernment is: after she was sawn apart [Nesirah] and took the six aforementioned discernments — one Dalet [door] and five other letters of MaNTzePaCh — during the six months of maidenhood, she afterwards returns with him Panim be Panim [face to face]. Then she is fit for a complete Zivug [coupling]. And then she, the Oral Torah, joins face to face with Zeir Anpin, who is called the Written Torah; and then she is called Bogeret [mature], which in Gematria is Torah [611], alluding to what was said.
